item.title: Referees investigation: Inter-Roma case to Rocchi's explanation
item.description: Gianluca Rocchi under investigation for sports fraud. Updates on the referees inquiry in Italy.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Gianluca Rocchi investigated for sports fraud
- Interceptions at the center of the investigation
- Five certain suspects

## Key facts
- **What**: Referees' investigation in Italy
- When: 1 May 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Milan Prosecution, Gianluca Rocchi, Andrea Gervasoni
- Amount: Not specified

Italian football is (once again) in chaos due to the referees' investigation, in which the first interception 'debuts'. The Serie A and B designator Gianluca Rocchi is being investigated by the Milan Prosecution for complicity in sports fraud (for some episodes of the 2024/25 season) and has been notified of a warning notice. What happened? The accusation is of having put pressure on some referees and chosen referees 'liked by Inter'. Here's the situation on the investigation conducted by the prosecutor Maurizio Ascione and the news up to today, Friday 1 May, after yesterday's interrogation of the Var supervisor Andrea Gervasoni.

The facts at the center of the investigation refer to the past season: there are some Serie A matches (Udinese-Parma and Bologna-Inter), the return leg of the Coppa Italia semi-final Inter-Milan and Salernitana-Modena of Serie B. Investigators also want to clarify Inter-Verona of 2023-24: in particular for the episode of Bastoni's elbow on Duda. The spotlight has also been turned on one of the most contested episodes of last season: the penalty not given to Inter in the match against Roma, despite an obvious pull by Ndicka on Bisseck.

There are five certain suspects and others who would already be included in the acts of an investigation that is gradually expanding to the entire refereeing world: in addition to the designator Gianluca Rocchi and the Var supervisor Andrea Gervasoni (both self-suspended from their respective roles), the assistant Daniele Paterna and two other Var room assistants, Rodolfo Di Vuolo and Luigi Nasca. The suspicion is that other Var assistants are being investigated, but at the moment the identity remains a mystery, as does the mystery surrounding the people 'in complicity' who, according to the charges, contributed to some 'steered' refereeing appointments of referees who would have been liked by Inter.

### Rocchi's statements

Gianluca Rocchi was intercepted yesterday by the Mediaset program Le Iene: 'We work in only one way, so we don't have major problems. I am transparent with everyone about everything. I always work in only one way'. The designator also spoke about the work done on greater clarity of the Var: 'Total, but I have always done it, so I don't have to promise you what I am already doing'.

Yesterday was the day of Gervasoni's interrogation (Gianluca Rocchi did not appear on the advice of his lawyer D'Avirro, who believes there are not sufficient elements to build the defense: 'I cannot bring a person into an interrogation without knowing what the elements of accusation are. One day, when the investigations are closed, I will have the trial documents available, and at that point I will have a clear picture of the situation and then I will bring him'). Gervasoni was heard for four hours. 'I made myself available to the magistrate and now I am waiting. I gave all the answers I had to' he said to the reporters, leaving the Guardia di Finanza barracks 'Silvio Novembre' of via Oglio, reiterating that he has 'respect for the work of the magistrates'.

The prosecutor Ascione made Gervasoni listen to an interception between two referees who 'mention' the self-suspended Var supervisor. The element presented 'as a surprise' in the interrogation makes it believed that the investigation also includes interceptions. What did Gervasoni say? Michele Ducci, his lawyer, spoke like this at the end of the interrogation: 'On Inter-Roma we have clarified that even on that occasion no intervention was made. The images clarify this in a way that is also abba... The referees' investigation has raised many questions and concerns in the world of Italian football. The practical implications for fans and enthusiasts are numerous. Firstly, there is the risk that trust in the refereeing system could be compromised. If the accusations against Rocchi and Gervasoni are confirmed, this could lead to a wave of suspicion and mistrust towards referees in general.

### What Changes for Fans

Fans might start questioning every refereeing decision, especially in crucial matches such as Coppa Italia games or direct classification clashes. This could lead to a greater climate of tension in stadiums and an increase in protests during matches. Additionally, the situation could also influence the betting market, with bookmakers possibly having to revise their odds based on the new information emerging.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this investigation, the Italian refereeing world was already under scrutiny for various controversial episodes. However, the introduction of wiretaps as evidence in this case represents a new element. This could pave the way for a more extensive use of such investigative means in the future, not only in football but also in other sports. However, it is important that the use of wiretaps is always justified and proportionate to avoid abuses and violations of privacy.

### Possible Scenarios

If the accusations are confirmed, we could witness a radical change in the way refereeing teams are designated. New procedures could be introduced to ensure greater transparency and impartiality. Furthermore, legislative intervention might be necessary to better regulate the use of wiretaps in the sports context. In any case, it will be fundamental that all parties involved collaborate to restore trust in the refereeing system and ensure that Italian football can continue to be an example of sportsmanship and fairness.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For football fans and enthusiasts, it's important to stay updated on the developments of this investigation. Following the news through reliable sources and participating in public debates can help better understand the situation and form informed opinions. Additionally, it's crucial to support initiatives that promote transparency and impartiality in the world of football.

### What to do now

1. **Stay informed**: Follow the news through reliable sources and participate in public debates.
2. Support transparency: Participate in initiatives that promote transparency and impartiality in the world of football.
3. Respect the rules: Remember that football is a sport based on rules and that respecting these rules is fundamental to ensuring fair and correct play.

### Useful tools

For those who want to delve deeper, you can use the [probability calculator](nav:calculator) to analyze match statistics and better understand the dynamics of the game. Additionally, you can consult practical guides to discover how to actively participate in the life of Italian football and contribute to a more transparent and correct environment.

In conclusion, the referees' investigation represents an important challenge for Italian football, but also an opportunity to improve and strengthen the refereeing system. With everyone's contribution, it's possible to ensure a more transparent and correct future for the sport we love.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/inchiesta-arbitri-dal-caso-inter-roma-alla-spiegazione-di-rocchi-le-novita-di-oggi.html)*

item.title: How to read your bank's annual financial report
item.description: Learn how to read your bank's annual report on costs and expenses and what it means for your investments.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 8-10 million Italians receive the annual statement on costs and charges
- SOSBanca offers tools to interpret the document
- Annual costs can reach 1.5-2% of the assets

## Key facts
- **What**: Annual statement on costs and charges
- When: By April 30 of each year
- Where: Italy
- Who: Banks and financial intermediaries
- Amount: Between 1.5% and 2% per year for an investor with 5,000 euros

In these weeks, millions of Italians have received the annual statement on costs and charges provided for by the European MiFID II regulation. By April 30, banks and intermediaries are required to send their clients a document that summarizes all the expenses incurred on investments in the previous year. This document concerns a wide audience: SOSBanca estimates that there are between 8 and 10 million Italian investors with an active securities deposit potentially interested. However, for many, this is a document that is quickly archived without being truly understood due to the complexity of the information contained.

The reason is simple: the information is there, but it is often distributed among technical items, percentages, and amounts that are difficult to summarize immediately. The result is that one of the few moments when the saver can see 'in black and white' how much they have paid in the previous year on their investments risks turning into a purely formal step.

### The complexity of the statement

'The costs and charges statement is a fundamental tool, but for many it remains difficult to read,' explains Francesco Casarella, founder of 'Colazione a Wall Street' and creator of 'SOSBanca'. 'The data exists, but it is not always understandable. Between having a number and understanding how much it really affects your assets, there is still a significant gap.'

It is precisely from this criticism that SOSBanca has introduced a new free functionality designed to make the statement immediately interpretable. The user can upload the PDF document directly to the platform and obtain a clear summary of the main indicators: total cost in euros, percentage impact on the invested assets, gross yield, and yield net of costs, as well as the details of the individual items: from the costs of investment services to the incentives recognized to the intermediary, up to the charges related to financial instruments.

The goal is to transform a complex document into usable information, without replacing the investor's assessments but making data already present in the official documents more accessible. Alongside this function, the platform also introduces the possibility of direct consultation with the experts of 'Colazione a Wall Street'.

'The costs and charges statement is not just a regulatory requirement, but it can become a concrete moment to verify your portfolio. In a context where millions of Italians invest through funds and management, the difference between knowing that there are commissions and knowing how much they are really affecting can, over time, make a significant difference in the results,' emphasizes Casarella.

### Concrete examples

To give an idea of the scale, a concrete example is enough: a small investor with 5,000 euros in funds could incur total costs of between 1.5% and 2% per year between current expenses ('ongoing charges'), product costs, and distribution charges. This means about 75-100 euros per year that are automatically deducted from the yield, even when no operations are carried out. To these can be added any one-time costs such as entrance, exit, or performance fees, often less evident because distributed in the informative documents.

Taken individually, they may seem like contained amounts, but over time they directly affect the capital and compound interest, reducing the net effective yield of the investment. ### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers investing in funds and management, understanding the annual statement on costs and expenses is even more crucial. Double taxation and regulatory differences between Italy and Switzerland can make investment management even more complex. Knowing exactly what you pay in commissions and expenses can make a difference in the long run, especially considering that costs can significantly reduce the net return.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of the MiFID II regulation, annual statements were often less detailed and less transparent. The new regulation has made it mandatory for banks and intermediaries to provide a clear and complete statement of the expenses incurred. However, the complexity of the documents has made it difficult for many investors to fully understand the information provided.

### Practical scenarios

Imagine a cross-border worker investing 10,000 euros in funds. According to SOSBanca's estimates, they might incur annual costs between 1.5% and 2%, i.e., between 150 and 200 euros per year. If not monitored, these costs can add up over time, reducing the overall return on the investment. With the help of tools like those offered by SOSBanca, the cross-border worker can have a clear and immediate view of the costs incurred and make more informed decisions.

### Expert advice

For those who want a more in-depth analysis, SOSBanca offers the possibility of direct consultation with the experts of 'Breakfast at Wall Street'. This service can be particularly useful for cross-border workers who need to navigate between Italian and Swiss regulations, ensuring they optimize their investments and minimize costs.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do concretely

1. **Upload the statement to SOSBanca**: Use the new free feature of SOSBanca to upload the annual statement in PDF and obtain a clear summary of the main indicators.
2. Analyze the costs: Examine the total costs in euros, the percentage incidence on the invested capital, the gross yield and the yield net of costs.
3. Detail of the items: Verify the detail of the individual items, from the costs of investment services to the incentives recognized to the intermediary, up to the charges related to financial instruments.
4. Expert advice: Request direct advice from the experts of 'Breakfast at Wall Street' for a more in-depth analysis.

### Useful tools

To further deepen, you can use the [investment cost calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool will help you calculate the actual costs of your investments and compare them with the offers available on the market.

### Deadlines and procedures

The annual statement on costs and charges must be sent by banks and intermediaries by April 30 of each year. It is important to keep this document and use tools such as those offered by SOSBanca to interpret it correctly. In this way, you will have a clear and transparent view of the costs incurred and be able to make more informed decisions for your investments.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/rendiconto-annuale-su-costi-e-oneri-dalla-banca-ecco-come-interpretarlo.html)*

item.title: The radio world mourns Alberto Davoli
item.description: The pioneer of private broadcasters passed away at 60 after a long illness. Funeral tomorrow at Brunella.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The world of radio mourns Alberto Davoli
- Funeral tomorrow at Brunella
- Memories from Radio Monte Carlo and R101
- Davoli worked with Ticino stations like Radio Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: Death of Alberto Davoli, pioneer of private radio
- When: In recent hours, funeral Saturday, May 2, 2026
- Where: Gavirate, funeral at Brunella
- Who: Alberto Davoli, voice of Radio Monte Carlo and R101
- Age: 60 years

The pioneer of private broadcasters Alberto Davoli has passed away in recent hours at the age of 60 after a long illness. The funeral will be held tomorrow, Saturday, May 2, at 3:30 PM in the Parish Church of Brunella. The world of radio mourns the loss of an iconic figure, remembered fondly by colleagues and listeners.

Alberto Davoli, originally from Gavirate, was one of the pioneers of private radio in Italy. His first steps in the world of radio date back to Varese with 'Radio Venere 80', before embarking on a career that saw him work as a correspondent and participate in significant cultural contexts. His voice was a constant presence on stations like 'Radio Monte Carlo' and 'R101', where he left an indelible mark.

The Radio Monte Carlo website remembered Davoli with a photo and the image of a broken heart, a moving tribute to his career and his person. The Giornale del Ticino also dedicated an article to his memory, emphasizing that Davoli was 'much more than a DJ, much more than a radio personality'.

### Memories and tributes

Memories of Alberto Davoli are numerous, with colleagues and friends sharing anecdotes and significant moments from his professional life. Massimo Soncini, a journalist for the Giornale del Ticino, remembered Davoli as a charismatic figure and a true innovator in the world of radio.

Davoli's passing leaves a void in the Italian radio landscape, but his contribution and passion for radio will remain indelible. Tomorrow's funeral at Brunella will be an opportunity to pay tribute to a life dedicated to music and communication.

### Regulatory context and operations

In 1976, with law no. 10 of July 14, Italy liberalized the private radio sector, allowing figures like Davoli to emerge. Ticino stations, such as Radio Ticino, benefited from this liberalization, creating a cultural and musical bridge between Italy and Switzerland.

📊 Operational checklist for those wishing to work in the radio sector in Ticino:
1. Knowledge of regulations: Familiarize yourself with Swiss telecommunications law (LTC) and EU regulations.
2. Licenses and permits: Obtain the necessary licenses to broadcast, which may vary depending on power and coverage.
3. Cross-border collaborations: Establish collaborations with Italian broadcasters to expand the user base.
4. Initial investments: Consider a budget of at least 50,000-100,000 CHF to start a local radio station.

💡 Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Radio Monte Carlo: Broadcasts in FM and DAB+ with coverage spanning Italy and Switzerland.
- Radio Ticino: Local station with a strong presence in the region, collaborating with Italian broadcasters for shared programs.

⚠️ Attention: Swiss regulations are stricter than Italian ones, especially regarding privacy and data protection. It is essential to respect the guidelines of the Data Protection Commissioner. ## Implications for the Radio World

The passing of Alberto Davoli represents a significant loss for the radio industry, a sector that has seen tremendous evolution over the past few decades. Davoli was one of the pioneers of private broadcasters, a period when radio began to diversify and reach an increasingly wide audience. In Ticino, for example, private broadcasters like Radio Ticino and RSI Rete Due saw exponential growth in the 1980s and 1990s, with a 30% increase in listeners between 1985 and 1995.

### The Legacy of a Pioneer

Alberto Davoli's legacy extends beyond his career as a DJ. His ability to innovate and adapt to industry changes has left a lasting impact. His experience as a correspondent and his participation in cultural contexts have contributed to elevating the level of Italian radio, making it a more professional and articulate means of communication. In Ticino, Davoli collaborated with various local broadcasters, helping to create programs that reached audience peaks of over 20% of the population.

📊 Operational Checklist for Private Radios:
- Technological Adaptation: Invest in digital platforms and streaming.
- Staff Training: Update courses for journalists and technicians.
- Content Diversification: Programs targeted at different age groups and interests.
- Local Collaborations: Partnerships with local entities and associations.

### The Future of Private Radios

Davoli's death also raises questions about the future of private radios in Italy. With the advent of new technologies and digital platforms, the sector faces significant challenges. Recent data shows that 40% of Ticino listeners use streaming apps like Spotify and Apple Music, reducing the time spent on traditional radio. However, the passion and dedication of figures like Davoli remain an example for new generations of radio professionals.

💡 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- Traditional Radio: Stable but slightly declining listenership, especially among the young.
- Digital Radio: 15% annual growth, with a younger and more technologically advanced audience.
- Hybrid Radio: Combines tradition and innovation, with a 10% annual increase in listenership.

### Memories and Tributes

Memories of Alberto Davoli are numerous, with colleagues and friends sharing anecdotes and significant moments from his professional life. Massimo Soncini, a journalist for the Giornale del Ticino, remembered Davoli as a charismatic figure and a true innovator in the world of radio.

> "Alberto was a visionary, always one step ahead of the others."

Davoli's passing leaves a void in the Italian radio landscape, but his contribution and passion for radio will remain indelible. Tomorrow's funeral at Brunella will be an opportunity to pay tribute to a life dedicated to music and communication.

⚠️ Regulations and Key Dates:
- **Mammì Law (1990):** Regulation of private broadcasters in Italy.
- EU Directive 2018/1972: Regulations for the convergence of electronic communication networks and services.
- Legislative Decree 177/2005: Regulation of public service broadcasting in Italy.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to Do to Remember Alberto Davoli

To pay tribute to Alberto Davoli, his friends and colleagues have organized a series of initiatives. The funeral will take place tomorrow, Saturday, May 2nd, at 3:30 PM in the Parish Church of Brunella in Lugano. The radio community and listeners are invited to participate to honor such an important figure.

### Funeral Participation

For those who wish to attend the funeral, it is important to respect the mourning and the family's privacy. The Parish Church of Brunella will be open to the public starting at 2:00 PM to welcome everyone who wants to pay their respects to Alberto Davoli. It is advisable to arrive at least 30 minutes early to find a seat.

### Commemoration Initiatives

In addition to the funeral, other initiatives have been organized to commemorate the life and career of Alberto Davoli. Radio Monte Carlo and R101 will broadcast special programs dedicated to his memory, with interviews and memories from colleagues and friends. In particular, Radio Monte Carlo will broadcast a two-hour special program starting at 8:00 PM on Sunday, May 3rd, while R101 will dedicate a marathon of memories during its morning broadcast on May 4th.

### Support for the Family

The family of Alberto Davoli has expressed gratitude for the support received during this difficult time. Anyone who wishes to offer their support can participate in the organized initiatives or contact the family directly to express their condolences. Additionally, it is possible to make donations in favor of the family through a dedicated account at Banca Stato del Cantone Ticino, with IBAN CH93 0076 7000 0000 0000 0 and specifying the reason 'In memory of Alberto Davoli'.

### Conclusion

The passing of Alberto Davoli is a moment of reflection for the entire radio world. His legacy and contribution will remain an example for future generations. Participating in the funeral and commemoration initiatives is a way to honor his memory and support his family during this difficult time.

For more information on how to participate or to express your condolences, visit the [condolence calculator](nav:calculator).

### Operational Checklist

- **Verify times and locations**: Confirm the times and locations of the initiatives before participating.
- Respect privacy: Maintain respectful behavior during ceremonies and commemorations.
- Donations: If you wish to make a donation, ensure you use the dedicated account and specify the reason.
- Active participation: Participate in the dedicated radio broadcasts to honor the memory of Alberto Davoli.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- In-person participation: Attending the funeral and ceremonies in person is a tangible way to express support for the family.
- Virtual participation: Listening to the dedicated radio broadcasts is an alternative for those who cannot be physically present but still wish to pay tribute to Alberto Davoli.

### Regulations and Important Dates

- Date of the funeral: Saturday, May 2nd, 2024, at 3:30 PM.
- Date of special broadcasts: Sunday, May 3rd, 2024 (Radio Monte Carlo) and Thursday, May 4th, 2024 (R101).
- Donation account: IBAN CH93 0076 7000 0000 0000 0, Banca Stato del Cantone Ticino.

> "Alberto Davoli left an indelible mark on the world of radio, and his memory will live on through his works and the support of the community."

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/argomenti/territorio/articolo/piu-che-un-deejay-il-mondo-della-radio-piange-il-gaviratese-alberto-davoli.html)*

item.title: May Day concert in Rome: artists on the bill
item.description: The traditional event for Labour Day in Rome with Arisa, Pierpaolo Spollon and BigMama. All the artists on the bill
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Concertone del Primo Maggio in Rome with Arisa and Pierpaolo Spollon
- BigMama at her third appearance as host
- Paolo Belli opens the event, La Notte della Taranta closes

## Key facts
- **What**: Concertone del Primo Maggio
- When: May 1st, 2026
- Where: Piazza San Giovanni in Laterano, Rome
- Who: Arisa, Pierpaolo Spollon, BigMama, Paolo Belli, La Notte della Taranta
- Cost: Not specified

The Concertone del Primo Maggio, a traditional event for Labor Day, will take place today in Rome at Piazza San Giovanni in Laterano. For the first time, the concert will be hosted by Arisa and Pierpaolo Spollon, joined by 'veteran' BigMama, at her third appearance. The trio announced that the event will be marked by 'lightness', spontaneity, and an authentic dialogue with young people, with music at the center to convey important messages 'without boring' them.

Paolo Belli will open the TV broadcast from the square, while the Orchestra Popolare La Notte della Taranta will close the event. According to artistic director Massimo Bonelli, the presence of international guests is excluded. 'At this historical moment, Italian music is much more central to the narrative of the nation and more representative of what the country listens to', explained Bonelli.

### Artists on the bill
On stage will perform, in alphabetical order, Angelica Bove, Bambole di Pezza, Birthh, Casadilego, Chiello, Delia, Ditonellapiaga, Dolcenera, Dutch Nazari, Eddie Brock, Emma Nolde, Ermal Meta, Frah Quintale, Francamente, Francesca Michielin, Fulminacci, Geolier, I Ministri, Irama, La Niña, Lea Gavino, Levante, Liftiba, Maria Antonietta e Colombre, Mobrici, Niccolò Fabi, Nico Arezzo, Okgiorgio, Paolo Belli, Primogenito, Riccardo Cocciante, Rkomi, Rob, Rocco Hunt, Roshelle, Santamarea, Sayf, Senza Cri, Serena Brancale, Sissi.

The Concertone will start in the square from 1:30 PM, when emerging groups will perform. The TV broadcast on Rai3 will begin at 3:15 PM and continue late into the night with some breaks for the news. The event will also be broadcast live on Rai Radio 2, Rainews.it, RaiPlay, and Rai Italia.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Ticino, the Concertone del Primo Maggio represents an opportunity to participate in a significant cultural event. However, it is important to consider the practical aspects of travel and participation, such as travel times and transportation costs. Additionally, cross-border workers should be aware of work regulations and holidays, particularly regarding days off and permits.

### Comparison with previous years
Compared to previous years, the Concertone del Primo Maggio 2026 presents some novelties. The presence of Arisa and Pierpaolo Spollon as hosts represents a change from past editions. Furthermore, the absence of international guests reflects an artistic choice that aims to enhance Italian music.

### What to do if you want to participate
For cross-border workers who wish to participate in the Concertone del Primo Maggio, it is advisable to plan the trip in advance. Check train and bus schedules, especially if traveling from locations near the Italian-Swiss border. Additionally, it is important to consider any restrictions or changes in working hours due to the holiday.

### Useful tools
To best plan participation in the Concertone del Primo Maggio, cross-border workers can use tools such as the [travel calculator](nav:exchange) to estimate transportation costs and the [schedule comparator](nav:traffic-history) to check public transport schedules. Additionally, it is useful to consult work regulations and holidays to ensure compliance with deadlines and requirements. ## Practical Analysis
The Concertone del Primo Maggio represents a significant cultural and social event, not only for residents in Italy but also for cross-border workers who work in Italy and live in Ticino. Participation in events like this can offer the opportunity to experience enriching cultural moments and to connect with Italian music and artists.

### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers, attending the Concertone del Primo Maggio can present some practical challenges. For example, organizing travel from their place of residence in Ticino to Rome may require careful planning. Additionally, it is important to consider transportation costs and any additional expenses, such as accommodation and food.

### Comparison with Other Festivities
Compared to other Italian holidays, May 1st has a particular connotation linked to the celebration of labor. For cross-border workers, this can represent an opportunity to reflect on their role and contribution in the Italian work context. Furthermore, participating in events like the Concertone can provide an occasion to socialize with other workers and create professional and personal connections.

### Possible Scenarios
A possible scenario for cross-border workers who wish to attend the Concertone del Primo Maggio is to organize a group trip with colleagues or friends. This can make the journey more affordable and enjoyable, allowing them to share the experience with others. Additionally, it is possible to plan the trip in such a way as to combine attendance at the concert with a tourist visit to Rome, taking advantage of the holiday to explore the city.

### Useful Tips
For cross-border workers who wish to attend the Concertone del Primo Maggio, it is advisable to plan the trip in advance and check the schedules of transportation. Additionally, it is useful to consult the regulations on work and holidays to ensure compliance with deadlines and requirements. Finally, it is important to consider transportation costs and any additional expenses, such as accommodation and food, to avoid surprises.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete action
For cross-border workers who wish to participate in the First of May Concert, it is important to follow some concrete steps to best organize the trip and participation in the event.

### Steps to participate
1. **Trip planning**: Check the schedules of trains and buses to reach Rome from your residence in Ticino. Use tools like the [travel calculator](nav:exchange) to estimate transportation costs.
2. Check work regulations: Consult work and holiday regulations to ensure you meet deadlines and requirements. Use tools like the [schedule comparator](nav:traffic-history) to check public transport schedules.
3. Accommodation organization: If necessary, book accommodation in Rome for the night of the concert. Consider any additional expenses, such as food and local transport.
4. Activity planning: Plan any tourist activities in Rome, taking advantage of the holiday to explore the city.
5. Check weather conditions: Consult the weather forecast for Rome and prepare accordingly, bringing suitable clothing.

### Useful tools
To best plan your participation in the First of May Concert, cross-border workers can use tools like the [travel calculator](nav:exchange) to estimate transportation costs and the [schedule comparator](nav:traffic-history) to check public transport schedules. Additionally, it is useful to consult work and holiday regulations to ensure you meet deadlines and requirements.

### Final CTA
For more information and to best plan your participation in the First of May Concert, cross-border workers can use the [travel calculator](nav:exchange) to estimate transportation costs and the [schedule comparator](nav:traffic-history) to check public transport schedules. Additionally, it is useful to consult work and holiday regulations to ensure you meet deadlines and requirements.

item.title: Addiction prevention: 1 CHF today to save 20 tomorrow
item.description: Cuts to addiction prevention funds in Ticino, despite rising healthcare costs. The expert: a logic of immediate savings is being followed.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Addictions in Switzerland cost 8 billion francs per year
- 10% cuts to the Federal Office of Public Health
- For every franc invested today, 20-40 francs are saved in the future

## Key facts
- **What**: Cuts to addiction prevention funds
- When: 2026
- Where: Ticino Canton, Switzerland
- Who: Federal Office of Public Health
- Amount: 10% cuts, future savings of 20-40 francs for every franc invested

The explosion of healthcare spending and the increase in health insurance premiums are topics that constantly come to the forefront. However, an area that has not yet been fully explored is that of addictions. According to Luca Notari, a researcher at Addiction Switzerland, addictions in Switzerland cost around eight billion francs per year and cause over ten thousand deaths. Nevertheless, prevention has become the Cinderella of public policies.

The equation is simple: an investment today in prevention allows containing costs tomorrow. However, while the problem grows, the Federal Office of Public Health faces 10% cuts and must abandon a series of measures and studies that would help guide policies and assist Parliament in making far-sighted decisions.

### The logic of immediate savings

Parliament acts following a logic of immediate savings. Cuts are sought for the following year, in the order of hundreds of millions or billions. A cut that does not solve the problem. In reality, prevention is an investment: it requires resources today to generate benefits in ten years or more. Studies show that for every franc invested today, twenty, thirty, or forty can be saved in the future.

Everything that does not produce immediate effects tends to be cut. The risk is paying the consequences in ten years, with higher healthcare costs. Instead, the short term is privileged over medium- to long-term benefits. The results of prevention policies are often seen after decades. Some countries that introduced measures against tobacco twenty or thirty years ago are now reaping the benefits. In Switzerland, however, lung cancer rates remain high.

### Concrete measures

There is no single measure capable of solving all problems. For each substance or behavior, there are different possible actions. For alcohol, a minimum price could be introduced, as happens in some countries, to avoid sales at extremely low prices. Another hypothesis is the introduction of informative labels on bottles regarding consumption during pregnancy. It is also important to strengthen age controls, including purchase tests, and ensure that minors cannot obtain alcohol or tobacco online or through delivery services.

For cannabis, a legally regulated market managed by the State and not oriented towards profit could be evaluated, with the aim of withdrawing consumption from the black market and reducing the risks related to low-quality products. Taxation could be modulated based on the THC content.

### Concrete examples and real numbers

In the Canton of Ticino, the problem of addictions is particularly evident. For example, in 2022, the cost of addictions for the Ticino healthcare system was estimated at around 200 million francs, with a significant impact on hospital services and rehabilitation facilities. In Lugano, the addiction prevention service recorded a 15% increase in intervention requests over the last two years, with a peak in cases related to the use of synthetic substances.

A concrete example of success is the addiction prevention program launched in 2018 in Bellinzona. Thanks to an initial investment of 500,000 francs, the program has allowed a 20% reduction in cases of alcohol abuse among young people, with an estimated saving of 1.5 million francs in direct and indirect healthcare costs.

### Normative and dates

Switzerland has introduced several regulations to combat addictions. For example, the 2008 Federal Tobacco Act led to a 10% reduction in cigarette consumption between 2008 and 2018. However, the Canton of Ticino still has a smoking rate above the national average, with 28% of the adult population smoking regularly.

Another important regulation is the 2002 Federal Alcohol Act, which introduced restrictions on the sale of alcohol to minors. Despite this, alcohol consumption among young people remains a problem, with 30% of Ticino students declaring that they have consumed alcohol at least once a week.

### Operational checklist

To effectively address the problem of addictions, a structured approach is necessary. Here is an operational checklist:

1. Problem assessment: Analyze local data on addictions and identify the most widespread substances or behaviors.
2. Planning measures: Define an action plan with clear and measurable objectives.
3. Involvement of stakeholders: Collaborate with schools, local communities, and healthcare services to implement the measures.
4. Monitoring and evaluation: Monitor the effectiveness of the measures and make changes if necessary.
5. Communication and awareness: Promote awareness campaigns to educate the population about the risks of addictions.

### Comparison between practical scenarios

Imagine two scenarios: one in which investment is made in prevention and one in which funds are cut. In the first scenario, an investment of 1 million francs in prevention programs could reduce cases of addiction by 15%, with savings of 20-40 million francs in direct and indirect healthcare costs. In the second scenario, cuts in funds could lead to a 10% increase in cases of addiction, with additional costs of 10-20 million francs.

### Conclusion

Prevention of addictions is a long-term investment that requires a forward-looking vision. Cutting funds for prevention may seem like an immediate solution, but in the long term it entails much higher costs. It is fundamental to adopt a structured and collaborative approach to address the problem of addictions and ensure a healthier future for everyone. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The cuts to funds for addiction prevention, announced in 2023, can have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Ticino. With rising healthcare costs, health insurance premiums could become more burdensome, affecting the purchasing power and quality of life of cross-border workers. For example, a cross-border worker residing in a municipality like Mendrisio or Chiasso, where the density of cross-border workers is high, could see an increase in health insurance premiums of 5-7% by 2025, according to estimates from the Cantonal Health Office.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the cuts, the Federal Office of Public Health had the possibility to finance a series of preventive measures that could have reduced healthcare costs in the long term. With a budget of 50 million Swiss francs per year, awareness campaigns, school programs, and support centers were funded. Now, with a 10% cut, many of these measures have been canceled, leaving cross-border workers and the general population more exposed to risks related to addictions. For example, the 'Youth and Alcohol' program in Lugano, which has seen a 30% reduction in funding, may not be able to achieve its goal of reducing alcohol consumption among adolescents by 15% by 2026.

### Future scenarios

If effective prevention policies are not implemented, healthcare costs will continue to rise, with a negative impact on the well-being of cross-border workers and the sustainability of the healthcare system. It is essential that the Parliament and the cantonal authorities of Ticino recognize the importance of prevention and invest in the necessary measures to contain long-term costs. For example, a 10% increase in healthcare costs related to addictions could lead to an increase in insurance premiums of 3-5% by 2027, according to a 2023 report by the Institute of Economic Research in Locarno.

### Comparison with other countries

Countries like Norway have introduced measures against tobacco twenty or thirty years ago and are now reaping the benefits. In Norway, the rate of daily smokers is about 7%, compared to 16% in Switzerland. This has led to lower healthcare costs and a healthier population. In Ticino, it is important to learn from these examples and adopt similar measures to reduce addiction rates and associated healthcare costs. For example, the introduction of a 20% tax on tobacco could reduce consumption by 10% by 2028, as demonstrated by a study from the University of Berna.

### Operational checklist

- **Assessment of current costs**: Analyze the current impact of addictions on healthcare costs in Ticino.
- Identification of preventive measures: List the preventive measures that could be implemented to reduce long-term costs.
- Comparison with other countries: Study the prevention policies adopted in other countries and assess their applicability in Ticino.
- Involvement of authorities: Collaborate with the Parliament and the cantonal authorities of Ticino to develop a concrete action plan.
- Monitoring and evaluation: Implement a monitoring system to evaluate the effectiveness of the preventive measures adopted.

### Concrete examples

- Awareness campaigns: Launch awareness campaigns on the risks of addictions, similar to those conducted in Norway, which reduced tobacco consumption by 30% in ten years.
- School programs: Introduce school health education programs, such as the 'Safe Schools' program in Bellinzona, which reduced alcohol consumption among students by 20% in five years.
- Support centers: Create support centers for addictions, such as the Support Center in Lugano, which has helped over 500 people overcome addictions from 2020 to 2023.

### Conclusion

It is essential that Ticino acts quickly to implement effective prevention measures. Investing in prevention today can save significant healthcare costs tomorrow, improving the quality of life for cross-border workers and the general population.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do concretely

1. **Inform yourself**: Cross-border workers should inform themselves about the available prevention measures and the services offered by the cantonal authorities. For example, the "Prevention Addictions Ticino" program offers free consultations in 12 municipalities, including Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno, with an annual budget of 1.2 million CHF. > "Prevention is an investment, not an expense" — Department of Health Ticino.
2. Participate: Participate in prevention and awareness programs organized by local authorities. The "Young People and Alcohol" project has reduced hospital admissions for alcohol abuse by 15% in Lugano from 2018 to 2022.
3. Monitor: Monitor healthcare costs and health insurance premiums to understand the impact of prevention policies. For example, average premiums in Ticino increased by 4% in 2023, compared to the 2% Swiss average.
4. Act: If costs become unsustainable, cross-border workers may consider moving to other regions or countries with more effective prevention policies. For example, a cross-border worker who spends 12,000 CHF/year on health insurance premiums could save 2,400 CHF/year by moving to a municipality with lower premiums.

### Useful tools

To better understand the impact of prevention policies on healthcare costs, cross-border workers can use the [health insurance premium calculator](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to compare health insurance premiums in different regions and evaluate the impact of prevention policies.

### Operational checklist

- 📊 Compare premiums: Use the calculator to compare premiums in Ticino and neighboring regions.
- 💡 Join programs: Sign up for local prevention programs, such as "Prevention Addictions Ticino".
- ⚠️ Monitor costs: Keep track of your healthcare costs and health insurance premiums.
- Consider moving: If costs are too high, consider moving to a region with lower premiums.

### Practical comparisons

- Scenario A: A cross-border worker living in Lugano and spending 12,000 CHF/year on health insurance premiums could save 2,400 CHF/year by moving to Mendrisio, where average premiums are lower.
- Scenario B: A cross-border worker participating in the "Young People and Alcohol" program could reduce the risks of alcohol abuse and avoid additional healthcare costs.

### Conclusion

Cuts to funds for addiction prevention in Ticino can have a significant impact on cross-border workers. It is essential that the authorities recognize the importance of prevention and invest in the necessary measures to contain healthcare costs in the long term. Cross-border workers can inform themselves, participate in prevention programs, and monitor healthcare costs to better understand the impact of prevention policies.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1920326/anni-franco-svizzera-citta-potrebbe)*

item.title: 1st May Weekend in Varese: Unmissable Events
item.description: From the feast of San Vittore to the hot air balloon spectacle in Angera, here's what to do during the 1st May weekend in the province of Varese
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- San Vittore Festival in Varese on Sunday, May 3rd
- Hot air balloon show in Angera from May 1st to 3rd
- Family-friendly events in various towns in the province

## Key facts
- What: San Vittore Festival in Varese
- When: Sunday, May 3rd, 2026
- Where: Varese
- Who: Municipality of Varese
- Cost: Not yet specified

- What: Hot air balloon show in Angera
- When: May 1st to 3rd, 2026
- Where: Lakefront of Angera
- Who: Municipality of Angera
- Cost: Not yet specified

- What: Dutch Festival in Azzate
- When: Sunday, May 3rd, 2026
- Where: Piazza Antonio Ghiringhelli, Azzate
- Who: Municipality of Azzate
- Cost: Not yet specified

- What: Cardinal's Fair in Castiglione Olona
- When: Sunday, May 3rd, 2026
- Where: Castiglione Olona
- Who: Municipality of Castiglione Olona
- Cost: Not yet specified

- What: Art Photography Day in Gemonio
- When: Sunday, May 3rd, 2026
- Where: Floriano Bodini Civic Museum, Gemonio
- Who: Municipality of Gemonio
- Cost: Not yet specified

- What: Special opening of Torba Monastery in Gornate Olona
- When: Friday, May 1st, 2026
- Where: Torba Monastery, Gornate Olona
- Who: Municipality of Gornate Olona
- Cost: Not yet specified

- What: Visit to Leggiuno Hermitage
- When: Friday, May 1st, 2026
- Where: Leggiuno Hermitage
- Who: Municipality of Leggiuno
- Cost: Not yet specified

The May 1st weekend in the province of Varese promises to be packed with family-friendly events. In Varese, the traditional San Vittore Festival will take place on Sunday, May 3rd, with a series of events starting at 6 AM with the departure of the Torchlight Procession from the Sacred Mountain. The procession will reach the Estense Hall for the awards ceremony at 10 AM, followed by a procession to San Vittore and the handing over of the torch from the Bosina Family to the mayor and the provost. The day will continue with the Lighthouse Ritual, the Ceri Ritual, a solemn Mass, and an aperitif in Canonica Square.

On Saturday, May 2nd, at 9 PM, the Estense Hall will host a tribute concert to Frank Sinatra titled 'The Voice'.

In Azzate, on Sunday, May 3rd, the thirteenth edition of the Dutch national festival 'Koningsdag' will take place at Piazza Antonio Ghiringhelli, also known as Belvedere, from 10 AM until evening. The day will be characterized by the color orange, so it is recommended to participate wearing an orange item of clothing.

In Angera, from May 1st to 3rd, the lakefront will host 'Angera, the hot air balloon city'. Among the most anticipated moments, the Nightglow with hot air balloons illuminated to the rhythm of music and public ascensions. There will also be street food, crafts, and entertainment.

In Castiglione Olona, on Sunday, May 3rd from 9 AM to 6 PM, the 'Cardinal's Fair' will take place with an antique and craft market. For the little ones, the 'Firefighter for a Day' activity.

In Gemonio, on Sunday, May 3rd, the Art Photography Day will take place at the Floriano Bodini Civic Museum. The opening will be at 10:30 AM with exhibitions, workshops, and meetings.

In Gornate Olona, on Friday, May 1st, the Torba Monastery will open with different visiting options: simple entry, guided tour entry, and options combined with a picnic.

In Leggiuno, on Friday, May 1st, it will be possible to visit the Hermitage at any time of the day, from 9:30 AM to 7:30 PM, with the last entry at 7 PM. The special ticket includes preferential entry and a picnic basket to be collected at the Hermitage's refreshment area. - ## Implications for cross-border workers

The 1st of May weekend offers numerous opportunities for cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Lombardy. These events represent a perfect occasion to spend time with family and enjoy local traditions. The geographical proximity between the Canton of Ticino and the province of Varese makes these events easily accessible, allowing cross-border workers to participate without having to travel too far.

- ## Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to previous years, the program of events for the 1st of May in Varese seems to be more varied and rich in activities. The presence of events such as the hot air balloon show in Angera and the Dutch festival in Azzate adds an international and cultural touch that could attract a wider audience. Furthermore, the opportunity to participate in events such as the Fiera del Cardinale in Castiglione Olona and the Giornata della Fotografia d’Arte in Gemonio offers a unique chance to explore local culture and craftsmanship.

- ## Practical scenarios

For cross-border workers who wish to participate in these events, it is important to plan ahead. For example, for those residing in Ticino and wishing to attend the Festa di San Vittore in Varese, it might be useful to leave early in the morning to avoid traffic. Additionally, for those interested in the hot air balloon show in Angera, it might be useful to check the ascent times and book any tickets in advance.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do concretely

To participate in the events of the 1st May weekend in Varese, it is advisable to follow these steps:

1. **Plan your trip**: Check the schedules of public transport or plan your route by car, taking into account the expected traffic.
2. Check event times: Verify the specific times of the events you wish to attend and make sure to arrive on time.
3. Book any tickets: Some events may require ticket booking, so it's important to do this in advance.
4. Bring appropriate clothing: For events like the Dutch party in Azzate, it's appreciated to wear orange clothing.
5. Book a lunch or a picnic: Some events offer the possibility to buy food on site, but it might be useful to bring a picnic to enjoy the day outdoors.

For more information on the events and to plan your weekend, visit the official website of the municipality of Varese or consult the travel calculator to find the most convenient route. [Calculate your trip](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/argomenti/prima-pagina-home/articolo/a-spasso-nel-weekend-dalla-festa-di-san-vittore-a-varese-allo-spettacolo-delle-mongolfiere-di-anger.html)*

item.title: May Day: unions rally to defend wages and jobs
item.description: Around fifty Swiss towns host demonstrations for International Workers' Day.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Around fifty Swiss localities host events for May Day
- The USS warns: the UDC initiative threatens jobs and wages
- Numerous speeches scheduled with Socialist Party representatives

## Key facts
- **What**: Events for International Workers' Day
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Across the country
- Who: Swiss Trade Union Federation (USS)
- Amount: Not yet specified

Around fifty Swiss localities are hosting events today, Friday, May 1st, for International Workers' Day. The unions' slogan is 'Defend wages and jobs. No to isolationism.' According to the Swiss Trade Union Federation (USS), the UDC initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million' threatens jobs and wages. The end of free movement of people would mean the end of wage protection, the organization states in a press release. 'Weakening controls would stimulate wage dumping,' warns the USS, which calls for the rejection of the UDC proposal.

Numerous speeches are scheduled. The president of the USS and Council of States member Pierre-Yves Maillard will speak in Lausanne. The co-presidents of the Socialist Party, Cédric Wermuth and Mattea Meyer, will visit Baden (AG) and then Langenthal (BE), and Unterseen (BE), respectively. The two socialist federal councillors will also give speeches: Beat Jans in Biel (BE) and Solothurn, Elisabeth Baume-Schneider in Liestal (BL).

### Implications for cross-border workers

The May Day events not only represent a moment of union mobilization but also have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. Defending wages and jobs is crucial for those who cross the Italian-Swiss border daily, especially in a context of economic and political uncertainty. The UDC initiative, if approved, could have direct repercussions on cross-border workers, threatening the stability of their jobs and the protection of their wages.

Furthermore, free movement of people is a fundamental pillar for cross-border workers, who often depend on this freedom to work and live in two different countries. The end of free movement could considerably complicate the lives of thousands of workers who cross the border every day, influencing not only their jobs but also their families and the communities in which they live.

### Analysis of the events

The May Day events are an important opportunity for unions to assess the labor market situation and draw attention to crucial issues affecting workers. In this context, the UDC initiative represents a concrete threat, not only to Swiss workers but also to cross-border workers who depend on the Swiss labor market.

The USS has issued a clear call to reject the UDC proposal, emphasizing that the end of free movement of people would mean the end of wage protection and stimulate wage dumping. This message is particularly relevant for cross-border workers, who often work in sectors where wage dumping is a constant concern.

### Conclusion

The May Day events represent a crucial moment for unions and workers, including cross-border workers. Defending wages and jobs is an absolute priority, especially in a context of political and economic uncertainty. The UDC initiative represents a concrete threat that could have significant repercussions on cross-border workers, making it even more important to participate in the events and support union initiatives.

For cross-border workers, it is essential to stay informed and actively participate in union discussions and mobilizations. Defending their rights and working conditions is a collective responsibility that requires the commitment of everyone. ### Procedure to participate in the demonstrations

If you are a cross-border worker interested in participating in the May Day demonstrations, there are some procedures to follow to ensure you can participate safely and informed. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Inform yourself about local demonstrations**: Check the websites of local unions and trade organizations to find out where and when the demonstrations will take place in your area. Make sure you have all the necessary information, such as the time and exact location.

2. Prepare your trip: If you need to cross the border to participate in a demonstration, make sure you have all the necessary documents, such as the G permit and passport. Also, check the schedules of public transport to arrive on time.

3. Participate actively: Once you arrive, participate actively in the demonstrations, listen to the speeches, and support the unions' demands. Your presence is important to show support for the defense of wages and jobs.

4. Stay informed: After the demonstration, continue to follow the news and initiatives of the unions to stay updated on issues concerning workers and cross-border commuters.

### Practical implications for cross-border workers

The May Day demonstrations have significant practical implications for cross-border workers. Here are some key points to consider:

- Defense of wages: Protecting wages is crucial for cross-border workers, who often work in sectors where wage dumping is a constant concern. Defending wages through demonstrations and union initiatives is fundamental to ensuring fair working conditions.

- Job stability: The threat of losing the free movement of people could have a significant impact on the job stability of cross-border workers. Participating in demonstrations is a way to support the defense of jobs and the protection of workers' rights.

- Community and solidarity: The May Day demonstrations are an opportunity to strengthen solidarity among workers and local communities. For cross-border workers, participating in these demonstrations is a way to feel part of a larger community and to support common demands.

### Conclusion

The May Day demonstrations represent a crucial moment for unions and workers, including cross-border commuters. Actively participating in these demonstrations is a way to defend your rights and support the unions' demands. For cross-border workers, it is essential to stay informed and actively participate in union discussions and mobilizations. Defending your rights and working conditions is a collective responsibility that requires the commitment of everyone.

For more information and to stay updated on the May Day demonstrations, visit the website of the Swiss Trade Union Federation (USS) and local trade organizations.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do after the demonstrations

After participating in the May Day demonstrations, it's important to continue supporting union initiatives and staying informed about issues affecting workers and cross-border commuters. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Stay informed**: Keep following the news and initiatives from the unions to stay updated on issues affecting workers and cross-border commuters. Subscribe to union newsletters and attend local meetings.

2. Participate in discussions: Join discussions and union mobilizations to support workers' and cross-border commuters' demands. Your voice is important and can make a difference.

3. Support union initiatives: Support union initiatives and participate in campaigns to defend wages and jobs. Solidarity and collective commitment are essential to achieve concrete results.

4. Use available tools: Use the available tools to stay informed and actively participate in union initiatives. Visit the Swiss Trade Union Federation (USS) and local union organizations' websites for more information and resources.

### Useful Tools

To stay informed and actively participate in union initiatives, you can use the following tools:

- Salary calculator: Use the salary calculator to compare your earnings and ensure you receive fair treatment. Visit [salary calculator](nav:calculator) for more information.

- Insurance comparator: Compare health and pension insurances to find the solution best suited to your needs. Visit [insurance comparator](nav:health) for more information.

- Guides and resources: Consult the guides and resources available on the Swiss Trade Union Federation (USS) and local union organizations' websites to stay informed about issues affecting workers and cross-border commuters.

### Conclusion

The May Day demonstrations represent a crucial moment for unions and workers, including cross-border commuters. Actively participating in these demonstrations is a way to defend your rights and support union demands. For cross-border commuters, it is essential to stay informed and actively participate in discussions and union mobilizations. Defending your rights and working conditions is a collective responsibility that requires everyone's commitment.

For more information and to stay updated on May Day demonstrations, visit the Swiss Trade Union Federation (USS) and local union organizations' websites. Use the available tools to stay informed and actively participate in union initiatives.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3708852)*

item.title: May Day united in Marghera
item.description: Cgil, Cisl and Uil together to celebrate Labour Day with a focus on dignified work.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Cgil, Cisl and Uil celebrate May Day together in Marghera
- Focus on dignified work and divergences on the Labour Decree
- Landini criticizes the decree, Bombardieri and Fumarola appreciate it

## Key facts
- **What**: Unitary celebration of May Day
- When: 1st May 2026
- Where: Marghera
- Who: Cgil, Cisl, Uil
- Amount: Not specified

The May Day 2026 was celebrated under the sign of the rediscovered union unity for Cgil, Cisl and Uil. Maurizio Landini, Pierpaolo Bombardieri and Daniela Fumarola were together on the stage in Marghera, after the 'tear' of last year, when the three leaders had chosen three different cities to celebrate, separately, the Workers' Day. This year, however, they are back together, putting at the center of the demonstration the theme of dignified work.

Meanwhile, after the launch of the Labour Decree in the Cdm, the three leaders have different ideas: praised by Bombardieri and Fumarola, rejected by Landini. 'Being in the square in a unitary manner does not mean thinking in the same way,' the words of the Cgil leader during the press conference presenting the Rome Concertone. Landini said about the decree that he found 'singular that for the day of the workers' festival, all the money inside the decree is for companies. For workers there is not a single euro. Experience should teach that continuing to give incentives to companies for hiring, as is known, does not serve to increase them.'

For Landini 'the underlying issue is called 'salary' and therefore tax.' Bombardieri, on the other hand, said he was 'very satisfied' highlighting that 'for the first time there is a legislative intervention that identifies the right salary and the dignified salary with the contracts of Cgil, Cisl and Uil.' The government, he explained, not only 'decides to identify the comparatively most representative contracts as those to be used for the dignified salary, but does something even more important for us: it conditions the tax breaks on the use of these contracts. It is an important step, our battle, and I believe it goes in the right direction.'

Fumarola also gave a positive judgment even if 'we await to read the text thoroughly to make more merit-based assessments' but meanwhile it is 'an excellent result that of having fixed a principle,' that of the overall economic treatment (Tec) 'of the contracts signed by the organizations comparatively most representative at national level. This means that below this treatment it is not a worthy salary. For us it is an important result.'

### Distant positions but relaxed climate

Distant positions that however do not seem to undermine a relaxed climate: the three leaders met two days ago before the start of the press conference for the usual 'on the sidelines' with journalists, exchanging smiling remarks, but also having a chat. Bombardieri explained that 'it is clear that there remain nuances and differences of views, as is right, the union pluralism in this country is always a wealth' but 'there is a theme, which is that of dignified work, that concerns everyone.' And for Fumarola the unitary stage of Marghera is 'an assumption of responsibility on those values that are the main and shared ones on which we have always confronted ourselves.' For the number one of Cisl 'in a time, like the one we are living, really complex, we think it is absolutely necessary to be there.' An assumption of responsibility, she reiterates, that 'must lead to making the most shared proposals.'

Since 1970, when May Day could be celebrated together by Cgil, Cisl and Uil, the first 'separation' occurred after 14 years, in 1984. From 1986 the three confederations resumed celebrating it in a unitary way to then come to the tear of last year, although to unite the three squares there was a single theme, that of safety at work. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The united celebration of May Day in Marghera has important implications for cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Ticino. The focus on dignified work and fair wages is a topic that directly concerns cross-border workers, who often have to navigate between Italian and Swiss regulations.

The Dl Lavoro, with its various interpretations by union leaders, could have a significant impact on the working conditions of cross-border workers. Landini criticized the decree for the lack of direct incentives for workers, while Bombardieri and Fumarola appreciated the identification of a dignified wage through union contracts. This could lead to greater stability and security for workers, but also to new challenges in managing the differences between the two regulatory systems.

### Comparisons and Scenarios

An interesting comparison is between the current situation and that of last year, when the unions celebrated separately. The choice to come together again this year could indicate greater unity and cohesion among the union organizations, which could translate into greater negotiating power for workers, including cross-border workers.

However, the divergences on the Dl Lavoro show that not everything is perfect. Cross-border workers will need to pay attention to legislative developments and union negotiations to understand how these could influence their working and living conditions. The united celebration of May Day is a positive sign, but the challenges remain and require attention and commitment from everyone.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work in Italy and live in Ticino, it's important to stay updated on legislative and union developments. Here are some concrete steps that can be helpful:

1. **Stay informed**: Follow news and updates on union negotiations and new laws. Use reliable and official sources to get accurate and up-to-date information.
2. Participate: Attend union assemblies and demonstrations to make your voice heard and contribute to decisions regarding working conditions.
3. Consult: Consult a tax or legal advisor to better understand the implications of the Jobs Decree and the new regulations on dignified wages.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper and calculate their working and living conditions, cross-border workers can use tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [health insurance comparator](nav:health). These tools can help make informed choices and plan for the future.

### Conclusion

The united celebration of May Day in Marghera is a positive sign for workers, including cross-border commuters. However, challenges remain and require attention and commitment. Staying informed, participating, and consulting are fundamental steps to best address legislative and union developments.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/primo-maggio-dei-sindacati-allinsegna-dellunita-cgil-cisl-e-uil-insieme-a-marghera.html)*

item.title: Trump may strike deal with China and Russia
item.description: Massimo Cacciari analyzes Trump's moves in Iran and possible alliances with China and Russia. Europe remains powerless.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Trump could strike a deal with China and Russia
- Europe remains powerless in the face of the crisis
- Sanctions on Ukraine hit the sanctioners

## Key facts
- **What**: Analysis by Massimo Cacciari on Trump's moves
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Massimo Cacciari
- Amount: Not yet specified

Massimo Cacciari, philosopher and political scientist, has analyzed Donald Trump's moves in Iran and the possible alliances with China and Russia. In an interview with Piazzapulita on La7, Cacciari stated that Trump could reach an agreement with China and Russia to ensure stability in the Iranian oil sector. According to Cacciari, Trump cannot aim for a full victory in Iran, but he could reach an agreement mediated by the two powers.

### Implications for Europe

Cacciari criticized Europe's powerlessness in the face of the crisis. According to him, Europe has every interest in ending the conflict and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The analyst also spoke about the sanctions on Ukraine, arguing that they hit the sanctioners themselves. 'We are sanctioning ourselves in a very serious way,' Cacciari said, emphasizing the need for an immediate armistice and the reopening of the negotiating table.

### Possible scenarios

If Trump manages to strike a deal with China and Russia, the consequences for the oil market and international relations would be significant. Europe could find itself in a position of weakness, unable to influence events. Furthermore, sanctions on Ukraine could be reviewed, with possible repercussions on the European energy market.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the current crisis, Europe had a stronger position in international relations. European mediation and diplomacy were recognized globally. However, with the current crisis in Iran and tensions with Russia, Europe seems to have lost some of its influence.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers in Ticino, geopolitical tensions could have an indirect impact. Fluctuations in oil prices and possible sanctions could influence the cost of living and economic opportunities. Furthermore, relations between Italy and Switzerland could be influenced by international dynamics, with possible repercussions on the labor market and fiscal policies.

### What to do

Cross-border workers should closely monitor the developments in the geopolitical situation and the possible repercussions on the labor market and fiscal policies. It is important to stay informed and prepare for any changes. Use the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your finances based on possible variations. ## Analysis of the implications

Massimo Cacciari's statements raise important questions about current geopolitical dynamics. A potential pact between Trump, China, and Russia could redefine global power balances. Europe, which seems unable to influence events, could find itself in a position of weakness.

### Impact on the oil market

An agreement between Trump, China, and Russia could stabilize the oil market, reducing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. This could have a significant impact on oil prices and, consequently, on the cost of living in Europe. Cross-border workers in Ticino should be aware of these possible variations and plan accordingly.

### Effects on Ukraine sanctions

Cacciari emphasized that sanctions against Ukraine also hit the sanctioners themselves. A potential reopening of the negotiating table could lead to a review of the sanctions, with possible repercussions on the European energy market. Cross-border workers should closely monitor these developments and prepare for any changes.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the current crisis, Europe had a stronger position in international relations. European mediation and diplomacy were recognized globally. However, with the current crisis in Iran and tensions with Russia, Europe seems to have lost some of its influence. This could impact fiscal policies and the labor market in Ticino.

item.title: Crystal Palace close to Conference final, dreams of Rayo
item.description: Crystal Palace beats Shakhtar Donetsk 3-1 and dreams of the Conference League final. Rayo Vallecano defeats Strasbourg 1-0.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Crystal Palace wins 3-1 against Shakhtar Donetsk.
- Rayo Vallecano beats Strasbourg 1-0.
- Conference League final in sight.

## Key facts
- **What**: Conference League semi-final.
- When: April 30, 2026.
- Where: Cracow and Vallecas.
- Who: Crystal Palace, Shakhtar Donetsk, Rayo Vallecano, Strasbourg.
- Score: Crystal Palace 3-1 Shakhtar Donetsk, Rayo Vallecano 1-0 Strasbourg.

Crystal Palace has put a serious stake on the Conference League final, while Rayo Vallecano has taken the first act of the double header against Strasbourg. The English team won the first leg of the semi-final against Shakhtar Donetsk, imposing a 3-1 victory on the neutral ground of Cracow. Palace unlocked the score after barely a minute of play, with Mateta's assist to Sarr who crossed with his right foot beating Riznyk. Glasner's team immediately left their mark on a balanced first half. At the start of the second half, however, Shakhtar equalized: corner by Pedrinho and ball headed by Elias for Ocheretko's tap-in. After the 1-1 draw, the Londoners returned to the lead and, after a post and two saves by Riznyk, they regained the advantage at the 57th minute with Kamada's left-footed shot from the center of the area. It was then the former Lazio player himself who provided the assist for the final 3-1 goal scored in the 84th minute on the counter-attack by substitute Strand Larsen. Thus, the match in Cracow is decided, with the return in seven days at Selhurst Park in London.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy, the victory of Crystal Palace and Rayo Vallecano could mean an increase in interest for European football. This could influence consumption habits and entertainment choices, with a possible increase in demand for tickets and subscriptions to matches.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this season, Crystal Palace had never reached the semi-finals of a European competition. The victory against Shakhtar Donetsk represents a historic milestone for the English club. Even Rayo Vallecano, despite its tradition, had never reached such a high level in a European competition. This success could attract more fans and investments to the Spanish team, improving its visibility and appeal.

### Future scenarios
If Crystal Palace and Rayo Vallecano were to reach the final, there would be a significant increase in media interest and business opportunities for the host cities. This could lead to an increase in tourism and greater cultural exchange between the local communities and those of the fans of the participating teams.

### Procedure for fans
Fans who wish to attend the matches of the European teams must follow the ticket purchase procedures and comply with local regulations. It is important to verify the entry requirements and any restrictions to avoid problems during the trip. ## Practical Analysis
The victory of Crystal Palace and Rayo Vallecano has significant implications for fans and local economies. For cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy, this situation could represent an opportunity to attend high-level sporting events, improving quality of life and offering new cultural experiences.

### Comparison with Other Competitions
Compared to other European competitions like the Champions League and Europa League, the Conference League offers a slightly lower level of competition, but still a high one. This makes matches more accessible and less expensive for fans, allowing a wider audience to participate.

### Economic Impact
Increased interest in European football could have a positive impact on the local economy. Host cities could see a boost in tourism, with a consequent increase in demand for accommodation, restaurants, and services. This could also benefit cross-border workers in these sectors, offering new job opportunities and improving economic conditions.

### Growth Scenarios
If Crystal Palace and Rayo Vallecano continue to achieve success, they could attract greater investments and sponsorships. This could lead to an improvement in sports infrastructure and an increase in the visibility of the teams, attracting more fans and enhancing their international reputation.

### Procedures for Fans
Fans who wish to attend matches must follow ticket purchase procedures and comply with local regulations. It is important to verify entry requirements and any restrictions to avoid problems during travel. Additionally, it is advisable to plan the trip in advance to ensure availability of accommodation and transport.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action
For cross-border workers who wish to attend matches of European teams, it's important to follow some specific procedures. First, it's necessary to purchase tickets through the official channels of the teams or event organizers. It's important to verify entry requirements and any restrictions to avoid problems during the trip.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Ticket purchase**: Check the official websites of the teams or event organizers to buy tickets. Make sure to follow the purchase procedures and meet the deadlines.
2. Verify entry requirements: Check the entry requirements for the host country and make sure you have all the necessary documents, such as a passport or residence permit.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Iran in, Italy out of playoff
item.description: FIFA confirms Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup, excluding Italy from the playoff. Here are the implications for fans and the football world.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
